conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, then I can guide you through the process.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 19 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 19 years old.

Since Alex is over 16, their eligibility depends on whether they are in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, full-time non-advanced education like A-levels, or approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education - he's doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! Since Alex is 19 and in approved education (A-levels), that's a good sign.

Now I need to ask about your relationship with Alex and your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, are you contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards his upkeep** (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old and in approved education (A-levels), which means you can continue to receive Child Benefit as long as he remains in approved education or training and is under 20
- You live with Alex in the UK, making you responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Important things to note:**
- Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex stays in approved education or training and remains under 20
- If Alex leaves education or training, you should notify HMRC
- If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim or continue receiving it if you're already claiming. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and currently doing A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (19 years old), who is in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 19 years old and currently doing A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (19 years old), who is in approved education (A-levels). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for him. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
